title: Dichromena tenella
taxonomy: Dichromena tenella is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is commonly known as the slender dichromena or dwarf saw grass. This plant is native to North and Central America. Dichromena tenella is classified as a type of sedge, which is a type of monocotyledonous plant. It is often found growing in wet environments, such as marshes and along streams.

intro: Dichromena tenella, also known as the dwarf hair grass, is a low-growing, annual grass-like plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dichromena tenella include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities.
